name: How long does it usually take to build or convert a site into something that’s truly AI-crawlable?

mainEntity.name: How long does it usually take to build or convert a site into something that’s truly AI-crawlable?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Most AI-crawlable website projects take 4–10 weeks from strategy to launch, depending on content volume, number of services/locations, and how quickly you provide information and approvals. Adding AI-friendly structure to an existing site can be faster.

Time isn’t just about design—it includes mapping real customer questions, writing answer-focused content, and implementing schema and internal links. Rushing these steps usually hurts AI visibility later.

Key factors:
- **Discovery and planning**: 1–2 weeks to audit your current site and define architecture.
- **Content and schema**: 2–5 weeks to write, edit, and mark up copy across core pages.
- **Development and QA**: 1–3 weeks to implement, test crawlability, and fix issues.
- **Your responsiveness**: Delays in feedback or content approvals extend timelines.

If you’re on an urgent timeline, prioritize a smaller, AI-ready set of pages first—home, main services, FAQs, and a couple of deep resources—then expand into secondary content after launch. Communicate any hard deadlines early so your team can scope realistically.

Treat AI crawlability as an ongoing process; even after launch, you’ll refine content based on how AI systems surface your site in answers.
acceptedAnswer.description: Building or converting a site to be AI-crawlable typically takes 4–10 weeks, including planning, content, schema, and technical QA. Smaller, focused projects can move faster, but ongoing refinement after launch is needed as AI search behavior evolves.
